system.aiNote: RAG over help-center articles + resolved tickets, GPT-4o for generation, text-embedding-3-small
system.multiSystemNote: React/Zendesk embed, FastAPI backend, retrieval service, Postgres/pgvector, OpenAI API, Zendesk API

verdictReason: Average of 2.0 plus four unresolved Blockers (PII to provider, no injection defense, no evals, no cost model).
executiveSummary: SupportCopilot is a sensibly-shaped RAG assistant embedded in Zendesk, but as a build document it is far from ready: the spec is a high-level sketch with no API schemas, no data model detail, and — most critically — none of the applied-AI engineering a production LLM feature needs. Customer PII is sent to OpenAI with no review of data-use terms or redaction, there is no evaluation strategy of any kind, and there is no cost model for a token-metered system. Close the four Blockers and flesh out the contracts before estimating or building.
strengths: The high-level RAG architecture (retrieve top-k context, ground the generation, human-in-the-loop edit before send) is the right pattern for this problem.
Embedding the assistant directly in the Zendesk agent console is a pragmatic, low-friction UX choice.
Keeping the agent in an edit-and-send loop rather than auto-replying is a sound safety default.

areas.name: Overview & Problem Framing
areas.assessment: The what and why are clear, but success is defined only qualitatively ("faster", "more consistent") with no measurable targets, and there are no non-goals to bound scope.
areas.suggestions: Add measurable success criteria (e.g. median draft-to-send time, agent acceptance rate, CSAT delta) and an explicit non-goals list (no auto-send, no customer-facing chatbot this phase).
areas.strengths: Problem and target user are stated plainly up front.

areas.name: Architecture & System Design
areas.assessment: A three-component decomposition is given in prose, but there is no architecture diagram, no failure-mode thinking (what happens when OpenAI or the retrieval service is down/slow), and no scale or latency targets.
areas.suggestions: Add a container diagram, define behavior when the model/retrieval dependency fails or times out, and state target latency and throughput.

areas.name: Feature Flows & System Sequencing
areas.assessment: Only the happy path is described, and only for Draft reply. Summarize and Tone have no flow at all, and no feature shows error/timeout/retry paths across the systems involved.
areas.suggestions: Add a sequence diagram per feature covering retrieval → prompt assembly → model call → post-processing → return, including timeout, retry, and partial-failure handling.

areas.name: Data Schema & Modeling
areas.assessment: The knowledge-base row is described loosely (text, embedding, source link) with no types, no embedding dimensions or index type, no PII classification on ticket data, and no retention policy.
areas.suggestions: Define the full schema with types and constraints, the pgvector index type and dimensions, a PII classification on ticket-derived data, and a retention/refresh policy.

areas.name: API Contracts
areas.assessment: Three endpoints are named with no request/response schemas, no auth, no error codes, no rate limits, and no idempotency. "All endpoints return JSON" is the entire contract.
areas.suggestions: Specify each endpoint's request/response schema with examples, the auth model, error responses with status codes, rate limits, and timeout/streaming behavior for the model call.

areas.name: Libraries, Frameworks & Tech Stack
areas.assessment: Reasonable libraries are named but none are version-pinned, LangChain is adopted with no rationale for the added abstraction, and provider lock-in to OpenAI is unacknowledged.
areas.suggestions: Pin versions, justify LangChain vs the raw SDK for this scope, and note the model-provider abstraction/lock-in strategy.
areas.strengths: Core stack choices (FastAPI, React, pgvector) are mainstream and fit the problem.

areas.name: Observability & Traceability
areas.assessment: Entirely absent. There is no mention of logging prompts, completions, tokens, latency, or cost, and no way to reconstruct what the model saw when an agent reports a bad draft.
areas.suggestions: Add structured per-call logging (prompt, completion, model+version, tokens, latency, cost) with request correlation through the pipeline and a PII-redaction policy for traces; name a tracing tool (Langfuse/LangSmith/Phoenix).

areas.name: Evaluation Strategy
areas.assessment: No evaluation strategy of any kind — no notion of "good enough", no dataset, no offline or online evals, and nothing gating prompt or model changes.
areas.suggestions: Define quality metrics (groundedness, helpfulness, tone match), build a golden set from resolved tickets, add a CI regression gate, and plan production sampling with human review.

areas.name: Feedback Loops & Continuous Improvement
areas.assessment: No feedback capture and no improvement loop. The rich implicit signal (how much the agent edited the draft before sending) is collected by neither the product nor any flywheel.
areas.suggestions: Capture accept/edit/reject and edit-distance as feedback, route it into the eval set, and define a prompt/model iteration and drift-monitoring cadence.

areas.name: AI Compliance, Safety & Governance
areas.assessment: Customer ticket content (likely containing PII) is sent to OpenAI with no review of the provider's data-use/retention terms, no redaction, and no defense against prompt injection from malicious ticket content.
areas.suggestions: Review and document OpenAI's data-use/zero-retention terms, redact PII before the model call where required, add prompt-injection defenses for untrusted ticket text, and map to GDPR obligations.

areas.name: Infrastructure & Environment Setup
areas.assessment: Environments are named, but there is no IaC, secrets are stored as plain environment variables, and there is no scaling, backup/DR, or OpenAI quota planning.
areas.suggestions: Adopt IaC, move secrets to a managed secret store with rotation, and add scaling, backup/DR, and provider rate-limit/quota planning.
areas.strengths: Separate staging and production environments are called for.

areas.name: CI/CD & Release Management
areas.assessment: Auto-deploy on push to main is the entire pipeline — no automated tests or quality gates, no rollback, no canary, and no feature flags for risky prompt/model changes that would hit all agents at once.
areas.suggestions: Add test and eval gates to the pipeline, a rollback procedure, canary/gradual rollout, and feature flags around prompt and model changes.

areas.name: Cost Projection
areas.assessment: No cost projection at all for a token-metered system — no volume assumptions, no LLM token math, no infra or third-party cost, and no unit economics or scaling view.
areas.suggestions: Build a cost model from stated volume assumptions: per-draft token math (input+output by model) plus embeddings, infra, and Zendesk/third-party costs, with per-ticket unit economics and a 1x/10x/100x scaling view.

findings.finding: Success criteria are qualitative only ("faster", "more consistent") — nothing measurable to build or test against.
findings.fix: Add target metrics: median draft-to-send time, draft acceptance rate, CSAT delta.

findings.finding: No non-goals stated, leaving scope unbounded (e.g. is auto-send or a customer-facing bot in scope?).
findings.fix: Add an explicit non-goals list.

findings.finding: No architecture diagram; the prose components are not visualized and boundaries are implicit.
findings.fix: Add a context/container diagram that matches the text.

findings.finding: No failure-mode design — behavior is undefined when OpenAI or the retrieval service is slow or down.
findings.fix: Define degradation/fallback and timeout behavior for each external dependency.

findings.finding: No latency, throughput, or availability targets.
findings.fix: State target p95 latency and expected concurrent agents.

findings.finding: Summarize and Tone features have no flow described; only Draft reply has a happy path.
findings.fix: Add an end-to-end flow per feature.

findings.finding: No error/timeout/retry/partial-failure path for any cross-system flow.
findings.fix: Add the unhappy paths to each sequence (retry, timeout, compensating action).

findings.finding: Knowledge-base schema is loose — no field types, embedding dimensions, or index type.
findings.fix: Specify types, vector dimensions, and the pgvector index (e.g. HNSW) with metadata fields.

findings.finding: Ticket data (likely PII) is stored and embedded with no sensitivity classification or retention policy.
findings.fix: Classify PII fields, define retention, and restrict access; gate before processing customer data.

findings.finding: No request/response schemas, auth, or error codes for any of the three endpoints — they cannot be coded against.
findings.fix: Define full contracts per endpoint with examples, auth, and status-coded errors.

findings.finding: No rate limiting or timeout/streaming behavior for the model-backed endpoints.
findings.fix: Specify rate limits, request timeouts, and whether drafts stream.

findings.finding: No version pinning; LangChain added with no rationale; OpenAI lock-in unacknowledged.
findings.fix: Pin versions, justify LangChain vs raw SDK, and note a provider-abstraction strategy.

findings.finding: No observability of model calls — prompts, completions, tokens, latency, and cost are not captured, so a bad draft cannot be debugged and cost cannot be tracked.
findings.fix: Add structured per-call logging with request correlation and a redaction policy.

findings.finding: No alerting on cost or quality regressions.
findings.fix: Add cost and quality dashboards/alerts, not just uptime.

findings.finding: No evaluation strategy — quality cannot be measured or regression-tested.
findings.fix: Define metrics and a golden dataset from resolved tickets.

findings.finding: Nothing gates prompt or model changes, so a change can silently degrade quality for all agents.
findings.fix: Add an eval regression gate to CI.

findings.finding: No feedback capture or improvement loop; the agent's edits before send (a strong quality signal) are discarded.
findings.fix: Capture accept/edit/reject + edit-distance and route into evals and prompt iteration.

findings.finding: Customer PII is sent to OpenAI with no review of data-use/retention terms and no redaction.
findings.fix: Review/zero-retention the provider terms, redact PII pre-call, document the legal basis.

findings.finding: No prompt-injection defense for untrusted ticket content that becomes part of the prompt.
findings.fix: Add input handling/guardrails and constrain what the model can act on.

findings.finding: Secrets stored as plain environment variables; no IaC, scaling, backup/DR, or provider quota plan.
findings.fix: Use a managed secret store with rotation; add IaC, scaling, DR, and quota planning.

findings.finding: Auto-deploy on push to main with no automated tests, eval gates, or rollback — a bad prompt/model change ships to everyone instantly.
findings.fix: Add test/eval gates, rollback, and canary rollout.

findings.finding: No feature flags for risky prompt/model changes.
findings.fix: Gate prompt and model changes behind flags for gradual rollout.

findings.finding: No running-cost projection for a token-metered system — LLM, infra, and third-party costs are all unestimated.
findings.fix: Build a token-math cost model with volume assumptions, unit economics, and a scaling view.

questions: Is there a companion API spec (OpenAPI) or data ERD that simply isn't referenced here? Several Blockers would soften if these exist.
Has anyone confirmed whether the OpenAI account is on a zero-retention / no-training-on-data tier? This determines the severity of the PII finding.
What is the expected ticket volume (drafts/day) and average thread length? Needed before any cost or scaling estimate.
nextActions: Close the four Blockers first: PII-to-provider terms + redaction (FND-018/FND-009), API contracts (FND-010), observability (FND-013).
Add an evaluation strategy and CI eval gate (FND-015/FND-016) before any model or prompt iteration.
Produce a token-based cost model with stated volume assumptions (FND-023).
Flesh out per-feature flows with error paths and add an architecture diagram (FND-003/FND-006/FND-007).
